strategy: Directly ground the end-of-third-quarter scoreboard visually near the known 120–126s anchor, then read the score text from the best frame. This is the minimal chain needed to distinguish among the answer options.

steps.purpose: Retrieve frames around the end of the third quarter where the scoreboard is visible, using the preprocess anchor near 120–126 seconds.

metadata.reason: Preprocess indicates scoreboard visible with 3 seconds left in the 3rd quarter in this interval.

arguments.query: basketball scoreboard showing period 3 ending and team scores

steps.purpose: Read the scoreboard text from the retrieved frames to extract both team scores and confirm the period/end-of-quarter context.

arguments.query: Read the scoreboard text, including both team names or sides, scores, game clock, and quarter/period indicator.

refinement_instructions: Use the new evidence only to resolve the answer-critical field: the score at the end of the third quarter and which team is leading or whether it is tied. Base the conclusion on the OCR-read scoreboard from frames near the end of period 3. Do not rely solely on preprocess wording if OCR is unclear; if the scoreboard text is not legible enough to distinguish 52-40 from 55-40 or to identify the leading side, leave the answer unresolved rather than guessing.
